source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A special light source directs 1 watt of light into a flat black ( $100 \\%$ absorbing) disc, which is mounted on an axle parallel to the beam (Fig. 2.84). The target starts to spin as it absorbs light. Can you modify the target so as to increase the torque above the values you calculated for cases (a) and (b)? For simplicity keep the target at normal incidence.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: place a quarter-wave plate before the surface of the mirror
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL
question mentions Fig. 2.84, but the record marks vision relevance as optional

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干为纯文字物理问答，没有显式引用图中位置、形状标注或视觉细节。
输入字段显示 requires_image=false 且 text_dominant=true。
虽有配图登记，但当前作答信息主要来自文字而非图像。
multi_step: 题干要求在已有 case (a) 和 (b) 结果基础上进一步提升 torque，至少涉及与前序结论的比较。
需要结合光吸收/反射/偏振与角动量传递机制来提出改造方案，不是单步事实回忆。
参考答案为具体光学元件改造（quarter-wave plate），表明求解依赖一定物理机制推理。
verifiability: 答案是短文本，形式上便于做字符串或语义匹配。
但题干依赖未给出的 cases (a) 和 (b) 上下文，单条样本独立核验难度较高。
问题表述为“Can you modify the target...”，可能存在不止一种合理改造，降低唯一可验证性。

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the sample retains core recoverable semantics.
The main risk is missing context from cases (a) and (b), which weakens standalone interpretation and verification but does not eliminate the core task.
The answer appears short-text and usable, yet the question is somewhat open-ended and may admit multiple valid modifications.
Metadata has an image-path formatting issue, but the actual image asset exists, so this is a quality concern rather than a fatal asset loss.

metadata.reasoning_chain: Place a quarter-wave plate before the surface of the mirror in case (b). Passing through the $\lambda / 4$ plate twice, the oncoming $L$ (or R)-handed circularly polarised light will be reflected back $R$ (or $L$ )-handed. This will increase the torque to $2 \tau_{0}$. No such enhancement is possible for case (a).
